meaning: vt.使适应(或习惯)新环境(或气候等),使服水土
examples: I hadn't had any time to acclimatize myself.
I help them acclimatize to living in the U.S.
It does take time to acclimatize, especially for guys who haven't grown up in an urban environment.
Television has the power to acculturate and acclimatize viewers to social change.
McCarthy hopes to avoid the pitfalls of the transfer period this month by ignoring overseas players, who often require additional time to acclimatize to the British game.
